Work_Story:
I work as a full-time Personal Secretary to a Director who manages three companies. These three companies are into yarn development, food production and legal management.
 
 My role is to give total support to the Director in all manner. I coordinate with all the three company managers and give day to day report to the Director about the functioning of the organization.
 
 Now talking about my duties, I arrange conference calls for the Director along with all company managers on a weekly basis and prepare minutes of the conference call and then prepare a final draft and dispatch it to all the people who were present in the conference call.
 
 I also hold meetings and call required employees for the meeting and coordinate with all the three companies so that the meeting should start on time without wasting Director's time.
 
 I take Dictaphone recordings for important Emails, Memos and Reminders, transcribe them and dispatch it to their respective recipients as per Director's instructions.
 
 Apart from that I prepare a diary for Director in which all important appointment records are stored. I manage the diary and do Calendar management according to the requirement of Director.
 
 I also keep petty cash records of the Director's expenses and send him an email time to time. I coordinate with the banking organizations and make sure that his credit card is working and ready to pay.
 
 I manage all payments of Utility bills, maintenance work of the organization. I also manage employee's advance cash requirement and coordinate with the accounts manager to deduct that as an Advance against salary from next salary cycle.
 
 I also look after the Milkman's payment, Grocery Store payment, Newspaper bill, etc.
 
 The Director was fond of food, hence he had kept several cooks. I used to manage the lunch and dinner requirement. The Director gives instructions on email what he would like to have today and I coordinate with the cooks to prepare it.
 
 I also used to look after cleanliness of office room, kitchen and bathroom and coordinate with the office Housemaid to keep them neat clean and tidy.
 
 I remind the Director about important appointment and events related to business promotion for all the three companies, etc. Then later on discuss it with the managers and report accordingly to the Director.
 
 I also used to book private cabs for important Employees and Managers whenever they come to meet the Director.
 
 Apart from that I also go to airports to receive foreign guests of the Director and arrange accommodation for them in a 5 star luxury hotel. Return tickets for all foreigners was managed by me.
 
 The Director is very fond of Dogs and hence he has kept Dog handlers to look after the Dog. I maintain a schedule for them to do Dog rounds 5 times a day. I also maintain a bath schedule for the dogs and the food which is going to be served as per Director's instructions.
 
 Apart from that I was also coordinating with the security guards for security of the premises. We do have a CCTV monitoring system. So sometimes I used to take surprise rounds to see whether the security guards are sleeping on duty or not.
 
 So this was all about my duties as a Personal Secretary.
 
 Thank you.
Overall_Why:
Yes I work extremely meaningful because I give my 100% effort to doesn't forget my duties. I use special apps on my smartphone which always remind of my duties and on what time to perform those duties. Technology has grown so fast that it had made human work much easier.
 
 A basic thinking is required and a responsibility should be understand in order to show yourself how meaningful you are. 
 
 Don't you think if there something not meaningful, then that thing is useless and just a waste of time and money. Nobody will keep such an employee who has no meaning for the organization and just a waste of money in terms of salary.
 
 I find my work meaningful because I enjoy it. Initially I was having lots of problems to manage things and I was getting messed up with several duties and forgetting things.
 
 The Director had warned me also that you are not doing your duties properly. I was worried that I will loose my job. Started thinking about and Googled on the internet "How to become a successful Personal Secretary".
 
 Got lots of ideas and now I had prepared up my mind that now I will do all my duties properly and will not give even a single chance to the Director.
 
 Things went pretty well for me afterwards and then later on I did it. 5 increments from the Director proved that I am the best person for this post.
 
 Started taking up more responsibilities and the Director had started understanding me very well.
 
 So this way my work is extremely meaningful for me.

Work_Story:
I work as a prep cook at a buffet which has locations around the country. The company has been downsizing over the years -- they have declared bankruptcy several times over the last decade.
 
 I began working there a little over five years ago. I intended this to be only a temporary job but due to unforeseen circumstances, I have remained there. At the moment I do not have much desire to change jobs because at least my job is familiar and stable. 
 
 I feel that I have the most demanding position in the restaurant. Not only do I have to complete my duties, but I also have to help the line cook open up on the weekdays. I still have not gotten used to this because I see this as an interruption in my day. I have to stop what I am doing and do another job for one and half hours, and then come back to my station and pick up where I left off. It forces me to divide my day in two. I have to prioritize and complete whatever I have to get done before we open. Often times I am juggling many things at the same time. 
 
 The other positions in the back of the house do not do this. They are given their lists and work at their own pace. Not only do I have to help out the cook, but my workload itself is more than anyone else's. I work non-stop in order to get out of there as fast as possible. Due to wage garnishment for unpaid debt, I have no incentive to want to work more because I can only make a certain amount of money per week -- the rest is garnished. I've learned something from this, though -- how to work quickly and efficiently. 
 
 My managers know I do a good job and let me do things my own way. One manager told me that he has been working in restaurants for over 30 years and I am one of the best workers he has worked with. I felt complimented. 
 
 My job is low-paying. I currently only earn a little more than minimum wage. Even if I did get a nice raise, though, I would earn more on my check, because of the wage garnishment. So I have not let that bother me too much.
 
 My daily tasks are varied -- for example, I have to make rice and then cool it down and then portion into containers. I have to cut up various vegetables. I have to make dressing and portion out green beans. On Friday and Saturdays I have to prep the breakfast items (because they are open for breakfast on the weekends). 
 
 I like to be organized and clean. I am constantly cleaning my area -- after every task, I wipe down my table, bring the dirty items to the dirty dish area. At the end of the day, I sweep and mop -- not only my area but the space around it. Nobody else will do this but I actually do not mind cleaning.
 
 Some of my coworkers have been working there for a very long time. Sometimes this scares me because I do not want to end up in the same situation. I see this as a temporary situation, and as soon as it makes sense for me to move on, I will. I do not want to wind up getting stuck in a dead-end job. 
 
 One things I like about my job is that I am able to work at my own pace. I am allowed to decide on the order which I carry out my work. I have my own work station so I am able to organize my area as I like it. I can keep it clean. It helps me concentrate and focus better when all clutter/unnecessary things are not crowding my area.
 
 All in all, I have a pretty thankless and demanding job, but I remain there because I am not in a position to make a change yet. At least I am familiar with it, and I like most of the people with whom I work.
Overall_Why:
To work as hard as I can gives me personal meaning because it is a good thing to work hard. Being lazy is not a virtue. Also, working hard at a dead-end job is meaningful to me. But overall, I do not feel my job is meaningful. I am there to perform tasks and to receive a check every two weeks. I do not interact with too many people at work. How meaningful can be to work in a kitchen? I am aware that almost anybody can perform the basic duties of the position, but on the other hand, not everyone can dedicate themselves to working hard at a thankless job. I find some meaning in this. 
 
 So, I would say that my work is personally meaningful because I apply myself to my work with dedication and commitment. I have only missed a few days in over five years. No matter how I am feeling, I am there because this is my job.
 
 In a general sense, I do not think my work is meaningful. It is not in my heart to do what I am doing. Ideally, I would like to be doing something very different. But due to my current situation, i am not able to be doing what I actually want to be doing. My job does not personally affect anyone. It does not contribute to society. Nobody's life is personally effected by my job. 
 
 However, I do attach some meaning to my work because it is a part, and has been a part of my life, for a long time. I am at least comfortable doing what I am doing and with the people that I work with.

Work_Story: My job is working as a special education paraprofessional. I work to assist the special education instructor for a fourth, fifth, and sixth grade class. I also interact with the special education first, second, and third grade as well throughout the day. My job in the morning is to check out books to students so that they may complete twenty minutes of their reading log each and every day. If they had checked out a book the day prior, I would check that book back in. The students are responsible for returning the books back in good condition and also putting the books back in the correct shelf. Afterwards we start our reading groups and the students who rotate to my group focus on reading comprehension. I have the students read a passage to me and then we annotate, take notes, and answer questions on the passage. We go over text features and vocabulary words as well. After groups the students go to recess and when they return I assist the instructor with social studies where the students learn to identify the main idea of an article or how to identify animal adaptations or learning about the Gold Rush. During this time they also practice annotating and forming two-column notes. We switch over to writing where we deploy kids from two other fourth grade classes. Some of my students move to the higher writing depending on their level. I remain with the instructor to teach the students who need the most help in all classes. I have students at my table were we go over annotating an article, taking two-column notes, forming a plan (and an opinion if the writing requires it), and then making a rough draft that must be peer edited before continuing on to the final draft. We work with each student to help them gain a better understanding on what draws the reader's attention (we call it the hook) and how to have a good point of view (teaching to start with phrases like "I think, I believe, I feel, In my opinion"). After writing groups the students go to lunch and as they return we start our math groups. I work with the students on material called Number Worlds where each group is doing something different depending on their level, but all groups are focusing on refreshing math concepts they have already learned. We take time in my group so the students may understand the concepts better and I often play math games related to the lesson as a reward. The students work out of a workbook after I give the lesson and using manipulative like fake money or ten sticks with one block cubes, the students get a hands on feel for how to trade or counting money quickly. At the last thirty minutes of the day our "little buddies", the first, second, and third grade special education class joins as the kids play blocks, matching games, and puzzles. This is a time for them to learn how to get along and resolve disputes in a healthy and safe environment. During this time I may copies for the teacher or play with the children to observe their social skills as well as intervene when necessary.
Overall_Why: It is a very meaningful job because the students at times get the moment of clarity where they finally understand a concept. It is the most rewarding thing to see. It is also meaningful because watching the students coming from feeling like they couldn't accomplish much to gaining so many different skills and being able to transition back in to general education is the most meaningful because the student feels an overwhelming sense of success. As the paraprofessional, I feel proud of the students to see how their hard work pays off. I also love to interact with the kids and try to be a positive role model since I work in an area where we see many students coming from broken homes. I find a lot of meaning when past students come to visit and still remember the things that I have taught them. It makes me feel like I am doing something right. The students also have a way of making you feel very loved by often giving compliments or making statements such as "you are my best friend". It is meaningful to know the students like you so much that they want to be around you and talk to you. Another reason why it is meaningful is because I feel in my position responsible for the education of the youth and that I want to inform them and instruct them the best way I can to set them up for future success after they leave the classroom.

Work_Story: I am working on {company}, As customer service executive past three years. My companies major job is testing product quality, so it's describes mostly depends on testing reports..usually we collect garment samples from supplier units,that samples has export quality and buying house nominated..first we provide test request form (TRF) to supplier that form contains list of quality checking tests, That tests categorized under some major departments for example 1. Wet, 2. Physical, 3.Chemical, 4.Analytical so customer select that tests which has required,Initially my lab book that samples with TRF and cut the samples and separate to each sections for testing purpose,Lab technicians check that samples before starting test, after that they test that sample step by step..every tests has taken equal time consumption and especially chemical test has take more time compare than another tests, and we follow some ISO Lab safety procedures like using gloves while testing, wear safety coats, wear safety shoes, and wear safety masks..these all are reduce sudden accidents..some chemicals made more skin affects cases so above safety materiel's more important things for Lab workers,after test finish every lab produce his result to technical verification team, That team persons specially trained for check that results internally and also result was very confidential and do not changeable,technical person only authorized person to change that result if it's wrong..Once that verification has finished result sheets move to data entery team for typing process,because results sheet not a proper method to deliver,it's have some buyer nominated templates (Ex: Font, Format, Units are variable) and data entry team enter report results with use of LIMS software (Laboratory And Information Management System) it's a internal server software for produce results with PDF format, All that report PDF's must have lab in-charge or Lab Manager signatory must with date,that finished PDF files move into Data analysis team, that team just verified typo error only,after finishing report hand over to key login team..Key login team means each persons handle separate buyers account like one person hand US countries,Another handles Europe countries,Key account person is a only person to communicate with supplier and buyer through the mail..and he also a responsible person to provide that reports on time and If he found any issues to deliver reports they must inform that issue and reason for delay to management and customer representative..All finished reports sent through mail only, and that PDF document must secure with password..it's prevent editing manually report, And key accounts get invoice from invoice generation team and sent that invoice along with reports..finally account team follow that customer for payment process, some customers categorized under Advance payment customers,That customers pay some advance amount to company for testing purpose,these customers reports must test and produce immediate criteria,finally all that reports kept safely and filed well for internal auditing and NIBL auditing,That NIBL auditing conduct as National level and once for two years..these step of procedure follow my work nature...please note all words written by me only,not cut copy from website...Thank you
Overall_Why: Yes, My work is very meaningful of all other works, because all that reports only comes to end with my efforts only, I am a correct person to communicate and describe detailed about that results and report to customer and buyer side,it's only improve my English and Communication knowledge much more..and all kind of issues only solve with my effort..sometime my working time get late to more than 13 hours but I don't worry about that..my word is "Dream big, to achieve Work Big".many jobs earn little in both cash and fulfillment. Low-paying service jobs-food prep, cashiers, fast-food cooks-were both poorly compensated and not meaningful (the chart's lower-left area). No professions at all fell in the chart's upper-left-hand reaches: highly remunerative and not very meaningful. Those employed in legal services (which includes lawyers in addition to paralegals, clerks, title examiners, etc.), into meaningful work can give us an idea of what meaningful work looks like, and how we can achieve this ourselves.That my pay is very low I ask many time to my management but they are won't be action but I can't leave my interest because this job only give more extreme meaningful.When you believe that the work you??re doing has meaning, it??s an extra shot of adrenaline. Good food for thought for anyone trying to create a workplace culture that engages employees,Even for those of us lucky enough to find all these aspects in our work, we don??t tend to feel meaningfulness as a consistent feeling. It??s more likely to be episodic, arising out of particularly challenging situations in which our skills and experience enable us to help others.

Work_Story: I work as an assistant at a company called {company}. I am an executive assistant to five different Managing Directors and about 7 other employees who work on the finance side of the office. I do a lot of different things for the office and for the guys that I work for (I say guys because yes, they are all men). I all around deliver them high-level support in anything that they might need from me. This even includes lunch and coffee runs. I also manage heavy and frequently changing calendars of senior employees. I book meetings for them as well as making sure that their guests are registered with the security team downstairs. I will set up catered lunches or whatever needs to be done in order to prepare for a meeting. I submit multifaceted expense reports, financial invoices, and reimbursements for these employees. Any company expenses that they need reimbursed needs to go through a long process, and I am the one who submits these expenses for them. I managed heavy multi-line phone traffic, as well as a large amount of email inquiries every day. I would guess that I probably get around 75 emails per day and probably a similar amount of phone calls. I arrange extensive travel plans and itineraries, including flight, hotel, car service, and restaurant reservations. Booking flights and hotels for other people is not as easy as it seems as each employee has his own preferences in terms of how they like to travel. It's also important that I stay within compliance rules when it comes to booking travel. I also coordinate and maintaine weekly attendance and annual vacation records for employees. More recently, I have started in assisting with the onboarding and offboarding of employees. I have their building passes created, I set up the accounts that they need in order to work, etc. This year, I also planned and executed our annual holiday party. It was much more difficult than I imagined to find venues, set up menus, stay within budget, and make sure that the event would be one that our employees had fun at! I also conducted internet research and put together detailed graphs and PowerPoint presentations related to the real estate market. I also have experience dealing with extremely confidential information. I also communicate weekly with overseas businesses for one of the Managing Directors that I work for and coordinate document signatures, notarization, and apostilles. I also do minute things around the office such as emptying the shredder, cleaning the kitchen, helping out the receptionist when she needs. I also send out a ton of certified mailings and FedEx deliveries on a daily basis. I assist in the company invoice retrievals, and also make sure that our company contact list is up to date. I email this out every three months. I also am in charge of covering for the other assistants when they are out on PTO or vacation. I will answer the phones of their guys, and any email correspondence will get directed to me.
Overall_Why: I do not find my work extremely meaningful because basically I am only doing things that help other people. I think that they could still get things done without me and that the work I do is relatively mundane compared to the large projects that people at this company take on. I am definitely at the bottom level of the totem pole and it is definitely discouraging at times. I feel like I have the capacity to be doing so much more, and much more meaningful work. I went to an excellent college, I think that I'm pretty intelligent, and I definitely do not want to be stuck in the role of an assistant for the rest of my life. I think I got here because I was anxious to get a job right out of college so I just took the first job that I could find that paid well. I think that this was a mistake and if I had spent more time searching for the RIGHT job, that I would be in a better position than I am right now, with a job that I felt was more meaningful than the job that I currently have. I also do not see any opportunity for growth at this company which is incredibly discouraging and also makes my work seem much less meaningful, considering I likely will not advance at all doing what I am doing. It is really tough sometimes to feel the way I do about my job.

Work_Story: The current position that I hold is as Customer Service Auditor. I really enjoy this position because it allows me to go behind the scenes and help companies to make sure that their customer service is working properly. In this position I have several roles. Sometimes I just go in and make purchases and see how long it takes for service to be rendered. Many times, this is for restaurants or companies that depend on timed service. Some companies have me go in and interact with their employees in a mean manner to see if their company standards are being met. I personally enjoy the bank audits the most. Most of them happen during a specified time period and require me to make some sort of financial inquiry of the banks services. These audits allow me to see the inner workings of many Fortune 500 companies while not being tied down to one company or position. Since I am contracted for each job, I can pick and choose which ones I want to go on. I personally hate doing some of the pizza shop audits because they are very particular in how they want pictures taken or they will not pay, which can mess up an auditor's reputation. In this position since it is contracted, an auditor's reputation is their business! Without the recommendation of the other audits it is hard to get big contracts that pay well. Some of these are simple enough where the auditor would just check the internal temperature of frozen food trucks and their inventory, but pay very well because it requires you to have special equipment and recommendations. Other companies want you to check on their competitors and tell them which store has a better inventory for the price. These usually require some sort of interaction with the employees and asking lots of questions. Audits can pay very well, however, they take a long time to submit payment. Payment for contracted workers can be anywhere between 30-45days depending on the company who orders the audit. This can put financial strains on those just starting out in the industry. However, this payment time frame can be offset by finding other means of filling in gaps. Often, I turn to Amazon for income fillers. Recently I became a recipe tester for Hello Fresh, Amazons official home delivery of fresh food shipped to your home, in convenient ready-made meals. However, when I test them at home, they are not kits already put together. I must take the spices they sent me and shop for the rest of the meal, follow the recipe directly and then take pictures. We use a special website for our reviews of the meals this helps to ensure everything is kept secret until the meals are revealed on the Hello Fresh site of Amazon. This position allows me to add approximately $100 per week to my income. I have tried to create my own career by doing these contracts of auditing and so far, it's very successful, especially since I am the only person in my area doing this. Due to this fact, many of the companies seek me out to perform audits for them monthly. Many have become permanent contracts. Making it so that my business can be successful.
Overall_Why: I think that my work can be very meaningful since it helps to ensure that not only are customers getting the quality that they are paying for but also to ensure that companies are getting the integrity from their workers that they expect. Many companies want to make sure their original ideas, goals and concepts are being produced in their satellite sites. Corporations often get complaints that can be followed up on with this matter in order to find out where their system is failing the customer. This type of auditing is the most effect and efficient to use because the auditor is not connected to the company and can give an unbiased opinion of the audit. If an auditor is connected to the company it is found that they can be less harsh in their judgement of the employee or store. Also, auditors who have had a bad experience with a company may be too harsh in their judgement of an audit. Due to this it is important that we have auditors who will do the audits exactly as they are so that any customer complaints can be established, verified, and make sure proper protocol is followed by the employee. Many companies believe this will establish a good rapore with their clientele.

Work_Story:
I work as an accountant in the cash control department. I make sure that deposits from Corporate accounting and the banks match the postings that are done on accounts in the billing systems. Every morning we download the bank statements and postings from the different billing systems and add them into an automated reconciliation tool that all other departments in the finance division use. 
 
 I also work on setting up ACH deposits so we can receive payments from insurance companies faster. I fill out the forms and take them to the treasury department to be signed off on by the cash manager. Once the treasury department has signed off on them they return the forms to me to be mailed, faxed, or emailed. I have to fix any problems that other departments report with their EFT's. If they received money from another department or payer into the wrong lockbox I have to determine why the mistake happened and fix it. I have to work with the insurance companies to make sure they have the correct information on file. 
 
 I work with the front end informing them that their batches are out of balance. I work with them to determine if they reported the deposit wrong or posted the wrong amount to the patients account. I work with the corporate accounting department to insure the amount they report actually matches the cash general ledger account. I spend a lot of time looking at large amount of data on excel spreadsheets and trying to present the information in a manner that makes sense to people unfamilar with what is going on. 
 
 I am also responsible for logging my department's continuous improvement ideas into the electronic Kaizen board so they can be shared with the rest of the enterprise. The best ideas are displayed to other departments around the building to show what accomplishments we have made and if we have saved time or money with our ideas. At times I lead the weekly Kaizen meetings where we review ideas submitted from other team members to know what we are all working on. We also talk about how we are doing as a department.
 
 I also make journal entries into the enterprise batch reconciliation tool to account for mistakes with deposits and postings from legacy systems that we don't pick up. I also have to mail checks out to the banks that come into the wrong area to be deposited into the correct account. 
 
 I also work with the financial analysts to explain why there are large variances in the Accounts Receivable accounts. I attend meetings with the IT department that supports the Enterprise Batch Reconciliation tool and try to determine new ways to use the tool so it is beneficial to other departments. I compile a list of checks that have not been posted and monitor what our newest aquisition is doing to make sure they are consistant with the other hospitals we have acquired. I work on any special projects that senior management has. 
 
 I also work with the internal audit department to pull any documentation they made need on deposits and posting files. I answer any of their questions they have for their work papers. I also fill in for any account representatives when they are out of the office to make sure the work gets done in a timely manner.
Overall_Why: I find my work meaningful. Many times through my research I am finding customers that have made payments in the office that were never posted to their account. They would continued getting billed for something they have already paid if it wasn't for me. This would lead to them very being very dissatisfied with the company because their billing was not correct. I also help locate mistakes in our bank deposits to make sure we aren't getting charged excess fees. It is important for me to get the ACH deposits set up so my company gets paid quicker and the insurance payments are applied to the patients accounts quicker. Many other departments depend on the enterprise batch reconciliation tool. It helps them know their bank batches are in balance and helps them locate any variances which saves the company money each year. I also work to make sure we will pass our internal audits before the external auditors come. Banks and other investors rely on our financial statements being accurate and my job plays a part in that. My job is on the back end of the patient's visit so it will be the last thing they remember if their billing is incorrect or if they end up going to bad debt for a bill that they already paid. I also monitor the front end patient service representatives to ensure they are processing patient's payments correctly and make sure they aren't stealing. What they report as a deposit should be posted to the patient's account.

Work_Story:
I work as a biological technician at a small company in {state}. Biological technician is a fancy way of saying that I spray weeds for a living. I work with the owner of the company and report directly to him. When he has more employees than just me, I become the crew foreman of a small spray team. It's not really a glorious job but it is a seasonal gig that pays well and lets me enjoy my winters on the mountains. 
 My day to day responsibilities are pretty straightforward; I drive out to the boss's house and prepare for the day. I gather any and all chemicals, protective equipment, and tools that I'll be needing for the day's work. I put everything in my spray truck and drive to the location of the job. This past summer I'd say I spent about 75% of my time at a super-fund restoration site on the Clark Fork river. It's going to become a state park over the next few years so they've been having a lot of work done with all different kinds of work, including weed management. Once I arrive at the site, I put on all of my PPE and fill my spray backpack with water, chemicals, and blue dye(which helps people see the treated areas so they know not to walk there). After I have everything ready, I work a certain area running a very tight grid while I hunt back and forth for the weeds that I'll be slaying that day. As for the weeds that I target, I spray a lot of leafy spurge, houndstongue, oxeye daisy, all sorts of different thistles, tansy and knapweed. Occasionally I'll even target invasive grasses like reed canary grass since it devastates wetlands. And that's what I'll do for 8-16 hours a day depending on the project. If I have crew members with me, we try to work together so we don't have much unintentional overlap and we can chat throughout the day. But when I'm alone, like I was for the majority of this past summer, I go through a lot of audiobooks.
 If the job calls for more of a mechanical treatment, I can use an atv to do the spraying. You can't really spot spray with an atv so it's really only worth using if you have a giant swath of land that needs to be covered or the job is so far from the road that you might as well save time by taking an ATV to do it. ATV work can get kind of sketchy because of gnarly terrain. Two summers ago I totaled my atv when I was climbing a steep bank, that usually causes me no problems, but it was the day after a heavy rain fall. I climbed the bank and when I got to the top of it the ground sort of just slid out from under me so I bailed and rolled out of the way before the atv slammed down to the ground. But that's about the most interesting thing to ever happen to me at work.
 I'm occasionally required to do some roadside spraying which is extremely boring. I drive along the side of the road at five miles per hour and do my best not to sleep. And every time my water runs out, I turn around and drive back to my water source. Two summers ago we had about 1200 miles of roadside spraying to do. At five miles per hour. You can do the math on that one if you'd like. But I think that just about wraps everything up.
Overall_Why:
I do think that my work is pretty meaningful. People hate weeds and I provide a service, rather my boss provides a service, that takes care of a specific problem for them. We take pictures before we start projects so that we can gauge our progress. But it's also in order to show proof that, yes, in fact everything is better off now than it was when we started, and therefore we are not breaching our contract. Some people just don't realize that weed management is a process that will probably take 5-10 years to get totally under control. When everything isn't coming back as grass the next year, they freak out and ask why they're paying so much for something that isn't working at all. Those kinds of people make the job a lot less satisfying. 
 But there are also the people that really fall in love with the way everything looks and they're ecstatic every time they see you out there. Those are the people that make the job worth it. Coming back summer after summer and seeing the progress that you keep making is a really great feeling. It's something tangible that you can feel good about doing. Unlike a sales job where you don't actually get to see the product that you just sold to someone in action, or a data entry job that you do over and over again, just to repeat it again the next day over and over. My job is always outside and it takes me on some great adventures and to some beautiful locations.

Work_Story: Well, what can I say? I have worked for Walmart for 14 years now. I have seen many things and many people come and go through out the years. Mind you that's not just customers, but also employees too. I started off as a cashier, but I am now currently working in customer service. I've been working in customer service now going on 12 years and absolutely love it. I wouldn't have it any other way. Yes,from the outside customer service looks boring and stressful. Honestly, at times it can be really tough. I love it because it is forever something different. With each customer comes a new problem to solve...even if it is as simple as processing a return. A lot of times the customer is already pissed off and very irrate by the time they get to the service desk. It is my job to make the happy & take care of them to the best of my ability within the policy. Luckily a good majority of the time, I'm able to please the customer & then they are off on there merry way. Not only are the off on their merry way, but a lot of times I have them smiling or laughing before they even leave. Although,I can't make every customer happy, but I try though. I have had people come in with items over $50 with no receipt. Sadly, I am not able to process these returns. Man, do they get really mad. Not much I can do about it though. In customer service there is more than just processing returns and exchanges. I would make sure all recall warning posters are up to date. I would separate all turns in to their designated department bins for those particular departments to come pick up. I would process the rentals and returns of rug doctors. Also, we have money services that is offered. I have learned to process money gram transfers & bill payments, utility bill payments, money orders, & so much more. When the store gets really busy, I will be pulled from the service desk to cover cashiers breaks and lunches. Sometimes the customer service managers would have me cover the door greeter lunch or break. Luckily since I am trained in multiple departments, I have covered in electronics, photo lab, vision center, sporting goods, garden center, and automotive. Even cooler is I know how to do work orders and make keys back in the automotive department. At my job, I am a jack of all trades. No job is too big or too small. Sometimes you will catch me outside pushing carts & cleaning the parking lot. I do what I can to help out and make our store presentable for everyone who comes to it. One day I will get promoted to customer service manager because of all the hard work I have put in. My ultimate goal is that I want to move up in the company. I can personally tell you that not all Walmart's are bad. My store is a decent one. I have wonderful coworkers and nice managers that will help me when I need it. I always try to learn every little bit more possible to be able to serve the customers better. I can see myself retiring from this company. Working at Walmart takes a special and very strong willed person. It is not for everyone.
Overall_Why: My job is beyond meaningful for me. I am a huge people person. I love a challange and every return gives me a challenge to solve. Sometimes it's small and some times it is big. I welcome it. I make a person happy with the customer service I have provided. Well, at least I try. I can't make them all happy. It's not just the customer aspect that makes my job meaningful. It's the coworkers and also for the fact it allows me to provide for my family. My coworkers support me by encouraging me to do better every day at work. Coworkers that come together when a coworkers family member dies or a coworker is having a baby. They come together as a whole and try to help console or congratulate. They have become a second family to my family. As for my actual at home family, they constantly encourage me to continue to strive to do better. They are proud of my accomplishments at work and are grateful for the work I do. As a company there are so many incentives that make my job so worth it. What makes it so worth it is helping someone who is having the hardest day with something not working or even can't find a certain item. It brings a smile to my face when they are so happy when I fixed then item or even called around to different Walmarts and found that item. Seeing them happy makes me happy.

Work_Story:
Currently, I work as an Editorial Assistant in the children's division at a book publishing company. I provide administrative and editorial support to the Publisher, Editorial Director, Executive Editor, and Editor at Large. I assist with 30-35 titles per season and manage manuscripts (young adult, middle grade, picture books, and board books) in all stages of the editorial and production process. I coordinate with Disney, Nickelodeon, Hasbro, and 20th Century Fox to obtain permissions for licensed projects and movie editions. I draft contract requests and put through check requests. I open and sort mail, make travel arrangements and appointments, maintain and update files and records, answer telephones, and takes messages as needed. I also assist other depatments, including the marketing; I mail out books for reviews and help set-up for book launch parties and other events.
 
 Before acquiring a book with my bosses, I have to read through many author and agent submissions before we narrow the submissions down to the best projects. Once all of our departments approve of the submission, we are able to go to the agent or author and offer to buy their book. Once the contract is signed, we are finally able to move forward and help authors further develop their books.
 
 The most important and interesting part of my job is when I work on the manuscripts before they are submitted to our managing editor for copyediting. I enjoy working with our authors on the actual story and character development of their work. I do this by reading through all of their drafts, writing editorial letters containing all of my suggestions for improvement, and then overseeing them to make sure that their revisions come back to us on time to hold the schedule. Once the story is done and ready to go, I submitted the manuscript to the managing editor who sends the material out for copyediting. Once it is finished being copyedited, I go over the changes with the author to make sure that they approve of all of the recommended updates. After this stage, I submit the manuscript to the design department so that they can get started on designing the book. In the meantime, I also collaborate with our design department of finding illustrators to do the inside and/or cover art. Once we pick someone and sign the contract, we submit ideas for the illustrations and work with the illustrator on the design of the book. Once the manuscript and the designs are all set, we submit the book to production who is in charge of getting us real samples of the book. Through this process, I am in charge of communicating with the author about all updates and changes being made to their book. Because this is a very personal project to them, I am also in charge of taking care of all of their issues and requests. Although we do own the rights to the book to make sure that it gets published, it is still the author's story in the end and I have to do everything that I can to make sure that their wants are met, while also sticking to what we think will make the book a success in the current market. As the book is getting ready to print, I coordinate with the marketing department to make sure that they have all of the necessary information they need about the authors in order to insure that they have all of the connections they need in order to promote the books. Once the book is published, the author and I no longer work together on a daily and weekly basis. However we still check-in to talk about the success of the book and if there may be any future books we can collaborate on together.
Overall_Why: My work is slightly meaningful because I am doing what I love, which is helping create books and put books out into the market that will help children read. What I don't find meaningful about my work is that we don't have much of a choice on what books we can acquire and which ones we can't. Because we need the approval of all of the different departments (and the approval of the CEO), we are not able to acquire and publish a lot of wonderful books that have a lot of potential. Instead, we have to buy books that we think are going to sell, whether or not we are truly passionate about the material. This keeps a lot of talented authors from being discovered by readers. This also makes my work not as much fun because I have to consistently work on books that I don't particular love or even like. I would be much more satisfied by my work if I was able to pick the books that we were going to publish, even without the full approval of everyone. If I think a book is going to be a success, that should be taken into more consideration. Another aspect of my work that isn't that meaningful, is that we can only focus on publishing children's fiction and there is no room to acquire adult fiction. I would like to be a part of finding adult books to put out into the market, but as of now I am only allowed to focus on a very narrow market.

Work_Story:
I get to the job site, a large construction project in Northern {state}, around 5:30pm. We do all the painting, fire coatings and all of the other special chemical coatings industrial projects like this require. First, I make sure all my guys have shown up or called in. After a quick roll call we stretch. We stretch our upper body, lower body, upper and lower back, then we try to get the legs again. We then look at the task load for the night and I figure out who is where and with who. We work six days a week, ten hours a day. I make sure any of the apprentices are spread out among more experienced guys including myself. We run through our safety check list whether we'll be on foot that day or using a scissor lift. Make sure all our safety gear is up to standards and each little crew has enough materials for their specific tasks. 
 
 I take each crew to spot by spot with their materials and make sure everyone understands what they are painting and what they are not painting. Or, if they are clean up, or maybe they're spotting for the painters, we have to work around other crews quite a bit so I make sure at least one guy is always keeping an eye on things surrounding our guys working.
 
 I spend the rest of my day bouncing from mini crew to mini crew replacing materials and/or tools and keeping plenty of water on hand at each task. I make sure the apprentices are involved and learning make sure answer any questions and/or demonstrate any paints, chemicals or tools they may be unsure of. I make sure everyone takes break together and leaves and comes back from lunch together. 
 
 I run the weekly safety meeting every Friday after lunch. We have a meeting every Friday and we bring over all the crews, our company is over 100 people strong. We discuss different techniques for certain chemical coatings as well as how to get any tough to reach places, ceiling corners for example, and still be safe. We're dealing with 30 foot ceilings in most of the building.
 
 I also make sure throughout the week to get paint on these coveralls and these glove covered hands. I make sure the guys know I might be one of the guys in charge but I'm right there and I'll do any job on site no matter how big or how "little". Keeping a sense of team and camaraderie is important to safety. 
 
 In another week we'll be starting to "finish paint" another huge section of office space including restrooms, both men's and women's. This kind of work requires a little more experienced touch and some good brush techniques. You don't want to miss something masking off a finished bathroom counter and mirror and find paint after you pull the paper. So, I make sure I have my been with me a while guys do the finish work As usual though they drag one or two apprentices along for the purpose of furthering the or skills at the trade.
 
 We check out at 4:00 am in the morning almost every morning unless we're behind on a specific part of the project. I make sure all my guys have everything clean, put away and and locked up and we go home.
Overall_Why:
I think it's only meaningful in the sense we are creating a place were thousands of men and women will come to support their families everyday. We are also taking the time to make sure apprentices are learning and growing in their respective trades so they can continue to support their families and loved ones.
 
 Aside from the dynamic of providing for the family unit I'm not sure there is really any deeper, extreme meaning to my work or a lot of other careers out there. Not to sound defensive, don't get me wrong, it's just that it earned the nickname "rat race" some time ago. And after a while we, or at least I, start to notice, maybe that title isn't so cynical. 
 
 So I guess if one wants to be philosophical about one's actual mechanism for feeding his family then we can all find deeper meaning at some level in our work. Beauty is after all, only in the eye of the beer holder. Unfortunately I realized some time ago if I was going to comfortably feed my family I would have to find spiritual fulfillment somewhere other than my work. Not to be or sound crass as I'm not trying to be. It's just that I have pondered about deeper meaning in my work before this survey came along. 
 
 And I came to the unfortunate conclusion that I unknowingly traded my desire to find fulfillment from my work on a deeper level to make sure I could provide for tiny little feet. And honestly regret not do I. I've found other ways to let my brain experience, wander, get lost and marvel at what life holds.

Work_Story:
I am a leasing consultant in a high rise building in the downtown area of {city}. I am employed full time and work Wednesday through Sunday. While my job can be stressful at times, I very much enjoy that every day is different. We seem to deal with everything imaginable at our building from birds getting in pipes and causing floods to 3rd party employees stealing money from our company. My absolute favorite part of work is being able to build relationships with our residents. We have 397 apartments which makes for a lot of different people (and many dogs) to get to know. My primary responsibility is taking prospective residents on tours of the building and helping them through the leasing process. I also deal with resident requests and am currently learning more of the assistant property manger role (invoices, resident complaint management, vendor management, etc). 
 
 We have a wonderful team at my building. There are only 5 of us and we are all pretty close and it is a great office atmosphere. We're very supportive of each other. Often times, the real estate/property management environment is very competitive. That is not how our team, or company for that matter, functions. We are always stepping up to help each other even when it means that the other person will make the commission and not ourselves. 
 
 The building itself is a wonderful place. We have the most beautiful southern views of the skyline of {city}. We are also only about 2 blocks from {lake} and have completely unobstructed views of the lake. This really is a huge selling point when prospects come for tours. You simply cannot get our views anywhere else in the city. 
 
 My corporate team is just as amazing as my direct team. They are also very supportive while motivating you to do well at your job. The company has strong values that they live by every day. It is a family owned company that has properties all over the US and internationally as well. They promote quickly and from within often. The industry tends to have a high turn over rate but from my experience this company has very good retention. Recently, my faith in my company was reaffirmed. We had a bit of a tragedy happen at the building over the New Year's weekend and our team stepped up and handled everything professionally and privately. Our HR department and one of the higher ups in the residential portfolio management team came to the building to personally make sure that the team was okay. They also are treating us to lunch and massages to make sure we have some time to relax. 
 
 Property management can be a bit crazy at times but I do not plan on changing industries any time in the foreseeable future. I studied criminal justice for my undergrad and stumbled into the industry but I could not be happier that I did. Helping to make sure people have an enjoyable and easy time while they are at home is very rewarding. It is amazing watching things come together with the different teams that are involved in the building. The front desk concierge and security teams will often relate things to the management office. We will then handle what we can or pass things on to our in house maintenance team. A high rise building works like a well oiled machine (or at least that's the hope) and without having capable hands in all of the teams involved there would definitely be some gears flying off.
Overall_Why:
I do find my work meaningful. Often times my job is to make people's lives easier while they are at home. Whether that be making sure the front desk is handling packages correctly, personally cleaning an apartment to make sure its ready for a move-in on time, or handling people's very personal problems. I believe it is a nice thing that (most) of our residents appreciate that there is always a smiling face waving to them through our giant glass office doors or a pleasant voice ready to help on the other side of the phone. 
 
 Many of our residents in the building are around my age. This makes it very easy to build relationships with them. I absolutely love when residents bring down pictures of big days in their lives like their wedding or bring us goodies just to say thank you (and because I love cookies). This shows me that while we may not be doctors or changing their lives in drastic ways, we are making a positive impact in a place where they spend much of their time - their home. 
 
 I also like being able to make the process of moving a little bit more enjoyable. Moving can be stressful, especially in a big city. I try very hard to take some of the stress off of our future residents and make it as easy as possible. Having residents happy when they move in sets them up to have a positive experience in their home for the next 12 to 18 months and that makes everyone involved a little bit happier!

Work_Story:
I am a Nurse. I work for a non-profit, faith based hospital in a metropolitan area near my home. I enjoy what I do very much. I chose this profession because I have always wanted to help impact another persons life. I feel as if I am doing that here. 
 The hospital I work for is a well respected facility, that was named one of the top 100 health care facilities in the nation. It makes me proud to be an employee there. My organization treats their employees with kindness, generosity, and respect. They offer a competitive benefit package, give you ample opportunity to advance within the company, and make you feel as if you are truly a part of a family there.
 Working as a nurse in a hospital, I never know what my day will consist of. Each day brings new patients, and with each new patient brings new requirements. Some days I may be doing nothing more than checking vital signs every hour, and other days I may be attempting to save someones life. The tasks I perform while working can vary tremendously depending on the patients needs. My job requires a lot of improvisation, and dedication. Here is a list of some of the job tasks I am required to do daily: intake patients, assess patient needs, monitor patient vital signs, collect blood specimens to send to lab, collect urine specimens to send to lab, disburse medications as necessary, evaluate and monitor EKG frequencies, perform life-saving skills such as CPR, or Cardiac Defibrillation, provide information to patients and families, correspond with necessary physicians in regards to patient needs, triage patients according to health risk severity, provide wound care, bath immobile patients, provide peritoneal care when necessary, assist patients with range of motion exercises, assist patient with physical therapy techniques, perform physical examinations on patients, assess patient care, interpret patient information, advise patients on health management and disease prevention, maintain sterile environments for patients, families, and medical staff... my list of job duties could continue on and on, but I think you get the picture. Being a nurse is a very diverse and taxing profession. It is also a very fulfilling position. Being able to be a small part of making a difference in someones life is an extremely intoxicating feeling. The adrenaline rush you experience after performing CPR and watching a patient come back to life is so rewarding. Of course, it can also be devastating if the outcome of your intervention is negative. I've experienced both in my career as a nurse, and losing a patient never gets easier. My favorite area to work is in the Obstetrics and Gynecology department. The physician I work with when I am on that rotation is a brilliant and intuitive physician, and there is nothing more exhilarating than being able to take part in bringing a new and innocent life into the world. The maternity ward at my facility is beautiful. It is full of kind, compassionate employees who work together to give the new parents and infant the greatest experience possible, and many times we succeed. Another job I really enjoy is the emergency room. It is fast paced, vigorous, and exciting. I have seen many interesting cases during my rotations in the Emergency Room. I have seen severely life threatening cases, and I have seen extremely simple cases. I enjoy the thrill of having to think on my feet and improvise for each individual case. There is something exciting about not knowing what to expect next.
Overall_Why: I do find my work extremely meaningful. Each day I am making a difference in someones life. I am working hard to help save lives, prevent spreading of diseases, and manage the care of any chronically ill individuals. Maintaining health care for individuals other than myself and my family is a very selfless act, and benefits all of society. My work is so important in maintaining the optimal health for each individual that I see. I am the liaison between the patient and the physician, and I am trusted to teach the individual patients things they wouldn't otherwise know about their health conditions. As a nurse, I influence the patient to want to care for themselves more, and to trust the physician to better care for them. I help daily to provide a healthier environment for our families and friends, to promote well being in human beings around the world, and to help create a healthier future for our children. In my opinion there is nothing more meaningful than helping to fulfill another's needs, and putting them before yourself. There is nothing more selfless. Each day, I focus on the health and safety of complete strangers, and each day I treat those individuals for who they are, unique and beautiful. Every human being on this earth deserves the best quality of life they can possibly have, and I work hard to help promote that. To me, my work is much more than just meaningful. It is necessary. It is beautiful.

Work_Story:
I work for a government utility that services 250,000 customers in a large tourist destination. I work in the Finance Department which includes Customer Service, Accounting, Purchasing and the utility warehouse. We work closely with our Information Technology department to keep everything working together to support the utility operations and fleet.
 My position is Analyst. I work with finance to develop reports. I work with customer service to oversee the data and reporting for all interactions by the call center agents. I work with accounting to verify data records through analysis. I work with purchasing and the warehouse to run reports and verify policy adherence.
 I work 5 days a week and only perform overtime duties when we reach the fiscal year end and during the financial audit time. I work Monday through Friday, days. I dress business casual and attend meetings, have small teams I work with and have work relationships with every department at the utility as well as the local county offices as necessary. I also attend the monthly public board meetings at the main county facility.
 I spend the largest amount of my time studying the daily and weekly data on customer charges and customer payments, selling building permit trends and evaluating growth. This is mostly a daily task because I need to locate issues with customer overpayments, duplicate and error so these can be resolved in the data before I begin my monthly report that is submitted to senior management and then to the board as well.
 It is also my responsibility to work on special projects which might include a review and recommended update to our guiding legal documents, working to create better business web site content, developing and updating customer service procedures or working with customer concerns.
 As the business upgrades its technology, I become involved in these projects. I work as a business analyst for most projects since I have a very strong familiarity with all departments, all the white collar worked and many of the blue-collar workers. Most staff are open and honest with me. When I work to develop requirements for these projects, I am able to get very good information about current process and suggestions for a new way of doing work.
 Of course, not all these things happen at the same time as we work from a schedule. I do have weekly and monthly reporting and every few months, I am asked to speak to at the supervisor's meeting and provide slides regarding the kinds of development permits lead to exposing, the number of new customers, the size of some our customer payments and any concerns that have caused spikes of work and effort in the call center.
 I will also accompany our public spokes person when we do information outings at colleges, parks and local events. I seldom speak at these things and act more as the living version of the events and trends for the organization.
 Finally, I will be assigned small teams that will report directly to me. I act as a leader/supervisor in order to most some processes that cross over department lines.
Overall_Why:
There are several reasons why I do find my work extremely meaningful. First, I am in water reclamation and believe very strongly that this represents the stewardship of our Green goals. The idea that water can be cleaned, put back into the environment and made available to create more jobs, homes and opportunities for everyone is really a thrill. I didn't understand this at all when I was first hired but as I became very familiar with the details of water cleaning, I became very proud of us all. And, I felt lucky to be there.
 Second and really very fulfilling is the actual work that I do connected to this. Because of my efforts to manage the reporting as I do, we are able to directly make a difference in what our customers must pay for this service. We have one of the lowest in the US and I feel very much connection to being involved in making this blessed service affordable for everyone. And, it is mandatory, so this makes me feel even better that the cost stays as low as possible.
 Finally, it feels extremely meaningful to be protecting the water of our nation. That is, what I support as a result of the laws passed in the Clean Water Act are direct enacted on my work desktop, so to speak. I can no think of any more meaningful work for some one like me that can make such a difference to an entire community.

Work_Story: I work for a government human services agency. I work in the quality control department, but the main functions of my job involve doing information technology tasks although I do not work in a formal information technology department. I design, develop, test, administrate and repair database systems for a majority of the functions throughout the agency. I also create statistical reports that are used to help management determine how our agency is placing among other agencies within my state. My typical day consists of taking in requests from fellow co-workers for tasks, working on scheduled weekly, monthly and quarterly reports as well as completing database assignments that I am given. The technology that I use for my everyday work includes software mostly from the Microsoft Office and 365 Suite. This includes programs include Microsoft Outlook, Microsoft Word, Microsoft Excel and Microsoft Access. All of our database systems are created using Microsoft Access. I have been employed in my current position for around 2 years. I am self taught for the skills that I need to do my job. I learned the skills needed through free online resources and some paid courses. The agency that I work for is involved with helping out the public through various public assistance means. Some of the programs that the agency has include but are not limited to food assistance, medical assistance, cash assistance, nursing home care assistance, adult protective services, and hospital care assistance. I develop solutions with the software available to me for the various areas of the agency. One of my largest tasks is to research and analyze the data that I receive from my databases and put it into readable business intelligence reports. I have been involved in creating many systems (using Microsoft Access) that are used to track agency work and attempt to approve on agency timeliness (as timeliness is a major focus in the public assistance realm). Many of these systems both track the work that we are doing as well as track how our agency staff is ranking in our agency accountabilities. I have recently been working on a new internal system that is going to be used to track how our case worker staff is doing on accuracy with completing many of their specific job duties. This system will focus mostly on our new hire staff as they are going through training, however it will be able to be used for ongoing staff as well. Most my projects typically only take a few days to work on, however when it comes to large tasks such as this new system it may take months for me to get a working product as I am the only one working on such tasks for this area of my agency. I am self directed in the work that I complete and must self manage all the tasks that come to me. To do this I use a calendar along with a spreadsheet and list high priority tasks before lower priority tasks. Along with the tasks I complete I am a everyday troubleshooter so if something happens to go wrong in one of my systems I am able to go over to my co-workers desk to help them with the issue and then push out global updates if necessary. All in all my main job duties are to be a supportive individual for all areas of my agency. This can be through database means or simple spreadsheet reports. My job is unique among the different jobs available at our the agency.
Overall_Why: I find it very meaningful as I hold a key role in my agency's performance amongst our state. If it were not for me there would be many systems that our agency would not have and management would not have as good of a grasps on the performance that we have compared to the rest of the state agencies. I am somewhat of a middle man for staff between the issues they have and the IT department itself as I perform many functions of the IT department without having much of the policy and red tape that staff has to go through to get IT to work on certain problems. I find my work meaningful as well because I can see the difference that I am making in my agency and the staff. I have seen time and time again the reports and systems that I've made make a difference in their work productivity ease of doing their jobs. I have been recognized by upper management a few times for the work that I have completed and it has confirmed that I am the right person for the job and that I am making a difference.

Work_Story:
I am currently employed in an Apartment Building doing maintenance. With maintenance work, I am responsible for general maintenance and repair work for the apartment complex. I keep things running smoothly and the wheels greased (literally). Being a maintenance worker requires a lot of troubleshooting abilities for a variety of different types of machinery and equipment like HVAC, electricity, plumbing and occasionally janitorial or lawn maintenance. I need to think on my feet, be physically strong and sometimes I get thrown in the deep end, by having to do things I have no previous experience or training for. 
 I need to to respond quickly, sometimes 24 hours a day, and troubleshoot using instruction manuals, blue prints and necessary tools. The job requires both indoor and outdoor work, and sometimes lifting heavy loads, so you'll need to be reasonably fit and agile. I am highly unfulfilled in my job. The company I work for rent out hundreds of apartments, thus there is a lot of workers employed. There is absolutely no room to grow and no recognition or sifting between hard and slacking workers. My supervisor is rarely working and leave us to do most of the work while she takes the credit. I have no problem with hard work, I feel like I go the extra mile in my workplace (even though I dislike it). I would like to know that my hard work is recognized and appreciated. I'm not even asking for raises or bonuses, a simple 'good job' from the boss would make my job so much more fulfilling. Because it is a high-end apartment area, the tenants treat us like 'peasants' and I don't appreciate management promoting this. When we had to work on Christmas, a lot of tenants complaint about us not shoveling snow fast enough. I mean it was Christmas, and we were working. Overtime is also a pain. Every second month each of us gets a turn to be on call. This is always a mess, there is absolutely no procedures in place when dealing with calls. There is no rules or standards for tenants. Tenants are able to call you in the middle of the night to replace a light bulb and you have to be back at work again at 5. I get multiple calls a night about tenants complaining about their neighbors or locking each other out of the house when fighting. Why should I, a maintenance worker, be expected to go and solve family disputes at one in the morning? The company also gives no options for investing or renting an apartment for a cheaper rent. My job description is very wide; winter time most of it goes to shoveling snow, then having to paint apartments out by myself, lifting appliances on your own, installing, doing electrical work, tiling, fixing pipes... so really I guess everything they can find for you to do without calling in a professional. The pay is also minimal, only $1700 a month. I would love to have a higher position, as I have very good managing and leadership skills. I have a bachelors degree in business management and I hope to put my knowledge in my work in the future.
Overall_Why: I do not find my work meaningful. I do not feel like I am contributing or making a difference of any sort in my current job. I like to grow, to grab opportunities and to work hard. It gets quite demeaning when you feel like you put in 200% but only get about 10% recognition for it. I get discouraged when I see high-income people treat lower-income workers like they are disposable and have no use for things other than minimal work. I believe you need to feel like the company you represent are aiming at making some sort of difference in their community, even if it is just using funds for good use or to spread some kind of awareness. I think big corporate business and in my case complexes promote majority, privileged people to co-exist in a vacuum of unrealistic truths. It is however very meaningful whenever someone occasionally does thank you sincerely for your efforts to help them. I think my job would have more meaning to me if my company created performance reports for their workers. I would like to be evaluated, in order to work on my shortcomings and improve my strengths. Extra benefits and praises for hard work will also be meaningful, even just the chance to work toward something. I think company policies and procedures will promote even the smallest jobs to be more meaningful. It is all about how you perceive your workers. I think I will be happy with my job if it was meaningul, even though the income is low.

Work_Story: My current job title is IT Support Specialist and I work for a healthcare company in central dolphin. My company has a few branches providing services in homecare for mentally disabled people, teaching under privileged preschoolers, and training service dogs that will go on to take care of disabled people or perhaps become bomb sniffing dogs! While my company is big and widespread, having about 3000 people located between 15 offices in 3 different states, I work in the administration office located in Harrisburg close to the state capitol. I work full-time hours and I am also responsible to work an on-call position after hours once every 2 months for about 2 weeks straight. At work I work in helpdesk position alongside 3 other technicians servicing all of the company??s day to day IT needs. During the day I will take calls from employees having problems with their computer or accounts, repair desktop and laptop hardware that has failed, and take inventory of all the equipment that comes in and goes out of the IT floor. I am also responsible for the creation and deletion of user accounts when an employee is first hired into my company or if they are leaving the company respectively. Over the past year I have setup over 1000 laptops and desktops and repaired over 300 different pieces of hardware that have come into my work area. I work with tools such as Active Directory and Microsoft Exchange on a daily basis to manage user accounts and emails, while also using Active Directory to ensure that computers that are connecting to our network and computers that my company owns. Because part of my company??s work is the healthcare of individuals and therefore their personal health information (PHI) we have to abide by HIPPA standards which means a lot to the IT department. We have to constantly make sure our networks and hardware are secure to store personal information on and part of my job is making sure that every computer is safe and secure both before they leave the IT department and after while they are deployed to homes. We do this in many ways, one is making sure computers are connected to our domain and our domain only accepts computers that are registered by an administrator account. We also use encryption on laptop hard drives to ensure if a piece of company property was ever stolen, any files that may contain personal information that was on the hard drive cannot be accessed without knowing the token phrase to decrypt the drive, which would take about 22 years using the strongest known computer in the world to brute force. Currently my work is deeply involved in setting up a new group of laptops with windows 10 as my company prepares to make the switch over from Windows 7. Overall my work environment is very laid back and employees are expected to work at their own pace while completing assigned tasks on a day to day basis. I enjoy that my work directly or indirectly affects helping people who may not be able to care for themselves by servicing the employees that care for those people. It is a humbling experience especially when making house calls to know that my work matters to the community and is helping to build up people who may not have ever had a chance to. I also enjoy the people around me as they have always been there to help me through any problem I may face both in work and personally. I have built long lasting relationships while at this job that I will carry with me through my entire life.
Overall_Why: Overall I would say my work is very meaningful. My company is involved in the healthcare of many individuals and its overall goal is to help the under privileged in society. We help mentally challenged people live a day to day life and help them build themselves to being responsible adults with their own social groups and activities and many times becoming employed in their community. Other than taking care of mentally challenged people, we are also involved in the training of dogs that will go out and service the community in a variety of ways. We have had a few dogs become FBI bomb sniffing dogs, other dogs becoming Seeing Eye dogs, and many other dogs becoming support dogs for the mentally challenged and for people with rare diseases like epilepsy. The other big thing that my company is involved in is teaching preschoolers of low income areas while also providing support to their parents. Being in the IT department, my work is involved in all of these areas as I am responsible in making sure all of our employees are able to complete these goals for my company. In the 21st century almost everything is done with a computer now, and so it is up to me to make sure technology works for the employee so that they can properly take care of the individuals they are assigned to. My work is also to insure that the people we do take care of also have all of their information protected and that they have the right to their privacy.

Work_Story:
I was working as research assistant in a neuropsychology department in major east coast city. The neuropsychology department was responsible for providing psychological rehabilitation for brain injured individuals who had accidents, stroke, heart attack, tumors, drug/alcohol related brain damage, and other diagnoses. 
 
 I had recently graduated with an associates degree in psychology and I was working for a group of Licensed PhD Psychologist. I would help them with entering data and gathering charts for their work with patients. I also, collected data from patients for the psychologists.
 
 Anyways, most of the psychologists on staff were very nice and friend, except this one elderly female who was a PhD in Psychology and she happened to be my supervisor. She was so rude she always harassed me and she was so moody and had an attitude. I was shocked she was a licensed therapist
 
 After starting the job, I was in a car accident and I broke my leg and suffered severe facial injuries, I was out of work for three weeks because of the extreme pain I was in and I needed to heal.
